item.title: 云成本对您的业务构成风险：这是保护自己的方法 | Cloud Costs Are a Risk to Your Business: Here’s How to Protect Yourself
item.description:
对于大流行后世界中的每个现代组织来说，云都是一项重要的预算项目。 但有时，它的成本变得太大而无法舒适。 一家价值 10 亿美元的私有软件公司告诉Andreessen Horowitz ，其公共云支出占总收入成本 (COR) 的 81% 。

虽然上面的例子是一个极端的例子，但它仍然反映了许多软件公司的挣扎。

如果他们首先处理好他们的云支出，也许我们目前看到的所有裁员都不会发生到这样的程度？ 在上面的示例中，人工成本不超过 19%，因此仅削减四分之一的云费用比让所有员工离开更容易节省。

幸运的是，有一条出路。 但让我们首先检查问题的根源。

为什么云会带来财务风险？

随着云使用量的增长并主导 IT 预算（预计到2025 年将达到 51% ），组织争相降低费用。 他们的第一步通常是预先购买特定数量和/或类型的云服务，以从折扣中受益（预留实例）。

但这样做时，公司基本上假设他们的许多业务变量在预订期间不会改变。

这往往与现实不符。 承诺的云支出通常比实际支出低约 20% ，一些公司最终超出了他们最初的估计 2 倍！

业界的故事证实了这一点。 在一个假期期间，Pinterest 的云支出超出了最初的估计约 2000 万美元。

因此，即使您通过承诺在一年或三年的合同中使用云资源来获得折扣，您也可能得不到足够的折扣。

或者，当人们在不再需要时忘记关闭资源或更糟糕的是，运行一项工作而忘记它时，宝贵的折扣容量可能会被浪费。 在任何一种情况下，您最终都会为无法转化为您的业务价值的资源付费。 接下来你知道的是，这些隐藏成本开始累积并成为重大的财务风险。

假设您正在运行一个本地数据中心。 如果您购买服务器，则需要固定成本和特定的运营费用。 您可以制定预算并坚持下去。

但是，当您开始使用 Amazon EC2 等公共云服务来运行您的应用程序时，预测成本会变得很棘手。 您的团队可以轻松地在给定时间点运行 100 或 1000 台服务器，这尤其具有挑战性，因为只需单击几下即可启动这些新虚拟机。

人们可能会为任务运行服务器并在工作完成后忘记它们。 但是你会继续为他们买单，积累大量的财务浪费。

定价模型的复杂性

大多数公共云提供商在多个页面和长表格中展示他们的定价。 使用不同的指标对服务收费，例如使用时间、数据量、计算能力和数据传输。

每个价格因素都会随着云区域、实例类型或整体数据量等维度发生变化。

最重要的是，云账单很长，所有成本数据都有延迟。 你在这里处理很多复杂性。

供应商锁定

许多公司最终只使用一个云提供商来处理多种工作负载，从而有效地将自己锁定在该提供商的定价中。 没问题，直到出现意外的价格上涨，比如 谷歌最近提高了定价。 其他供应商也可能效仿。

供应商锁定会给您带来许多不必要的问题，从替代服务中错失的机会到您正在使用的解决方案被淘汰的风险。

出路

如果您想让您的公司在未来抵御这些风险，请从降低运营成本开始。 公共云费用是一个很好的选择，因为市场上到处都是解决方案，可以帮助削减您的云费用，而无需付出太多额外的努力。

通过自动化与管理和优化云成本相关的所有任务，您可以重新控制资源，而无需雇用额外的工程师。



原文:

The cloud makes a big budget item for every modern organization in the post-pandemic world. But sometimes, its costs become too big for comfort. A billion-dollar private software company told Andreessen Horowitz that its public cloud spend made up 81% of the total cost of revenue (COR).

While the example above is an extreme case, it still reflects the struggle of many software companies. 

Perhaps all the layoffs we’re currently seeing wouldn’t be happening to such an extent if they took care of their cloud spend first? In the example above, labor cost is not more than 19%, so cutting a mere quarter of the cloud bill would easily save more than letting their entire staff go. 

Luckily, there’s a way out. But let’s examine the root of the problem first.

Why does the cloud come with a financial risk?

As their cloud usage grows and dominates IT budgets (expected to hit 51% by 2025), organizations scramble to lower their bills. Their first move is often to pre-purchase a specific amount and/or type of cloud services to benefit from discounts (reserved instances). 

But when doing that, companies essentially assume many of their business variables won’t change during the reservation period. 

This often doesn’t match the reality. Committed cloud spend is usually ~20% lower than actual spend, and some companies end up going over their initial estimations by 2x!

Stories from the industry confirm this. During one holiday season, Pinterest’s cloud spend went beyond the initial estimates by some $20 million.

So, even if you snatch a discount by committing to use cloud resources within a one- or three-year contract, you might not get enough of it. 

Or the precious discounted capacity might get wasted when people forget to shut resources down when no longer needed or worse, run a job and forget about it. That happened to Adobe which racked up an unplanned cloud bill of over 500k because of a computing workload left running on Azure. 

In either case, you end up paying for resources that don’t translate into any value for your business. Next thing you know, these hidden costs start adding up and become a significant financial risk.

3 biggest cloud cost risks

Imagine that you’re running an on-premises data center. If you buy a server, it comes with a fixed cost and specific operating expense. You can create your budget and stick to it.

But predicting costs gets tricky when you start using public cloud services like Amazon EC2 to run your applications. Your team can easily run 100 or 1000 servers at a given point in time, which is especially challenging because spinning up these new virtual machines takes a few clicks. 

People might run servers for tasks and forget about them once the job is done. But you’ll keep paying for them, accumulating massive financial waste. 

The complexity of pricing models

Most public cloud providers present their pricing across multiple pages and long tables. Services are charged using different metrics like time of use, data volumes, compute capacity, and data transfer.

And each price factor changes across dimensions like cloud region, instance type, or overall data volume. 

On top of that, cloud bills are long, and all the cost data comes with a delay. You’re dealing with a lot of complexity here.

Vendor lock-in

Many companies end up using only one cloud provider for multiple workloads, effectively locking themselves into the pricing of that provider. No issue, until a surprise price hike happens, like Google raising its pricing recently. And other providers might as well follow.

Vendor lock-in brings you many unwanted issues, from missed opportunities in alternative services to the risk of having the solution you’re using sunsetted. 

The way out

If you want to future-proof your company against these risks, start by reducing your operating costs. Public cloud expenses are a great candidate for this because the market is full of solutions that can help slash your cloud bill without much extra effort.

By automating all the tasks related to managing and optimizing your cloud costs, you gain back control over your resources without having to hire additional engineers.
item.author: castai

item.title: 阅读清单：早期职业版 | Reading List: The Early Career Edition
item.description:
我职业生涯的大部分时间都在教授大学英语。 我读了这么多小说、诗歌、戏剧和剧本。 当我进入科技行业时，我有一段时间停止了阅读。 我正在处理创伤。 我离开了一个不支持我的职业。 我是一个 4 岁的妈妈，我很累。 我曾经认为我是一个视觉学习者。 但我不能再坐着看书了。 我讨厌听有声读物。 但是当我在科技行业找到第一份工作时，我给自己买了一副 Airpods 来庆祝。 我发现我喜欢听播客和有声读物，但只能用耳机。 从那时起，我就开始大量食用它们。 但我的口味发生了变化。 我已经进入了一个新的行业——实际上是两种：技术和社区——所以我的阅读清单反映了这一点。 我很感激我们的公共图书馆使我能够寻求一条探索之路，一种适合我和我的需求的教育，并促成了这篇文章。 今天，我将为新的和早期的职业开发者分解我的前五种阅读资源。

激进的坦率：在不失去人性的情况下做一个超棒的老板金·马龙·斯科特

作为一名新开发人员，您将收到反馈，并且您还需要知道如何提供反馈。 经常会很难。 随着您的进步，了解反馈的重要性，以及对同理心和行动意图的诚实将是非常重要的。 获得良好的反馈可以让您成长。 提供良好的反馈可以使他人成长。 这本书可以帮助你做到这两点。

我也听过其他类似的书，但是这本让我印象深刻。 也许是因为他经常谈论技术。 也许是因为让自己沉浸在一件事中的想法引起了我的共鸣。 但它说明了职业转变的旅程以及您在此过程中会遇到的困难。

无聊和聪明：如何间隔开来释放你最具生产力和创造力的自我Manoush Zomorodi

这是我最近听的一首。 它验证了我忘记的一个想法。 如此多的闯入技术是 24-7 技术。 学习技术。 关于科技的爱好。 关于技术的附带项目。 我去了一个训练营，尽管我做了一年多，但我的思想一直停留在技术上。 我很难打破那种我需要一直保持联系的感觉。 然而，当我远离电脑时，我的一些最重要的、充满成长的经历已经到来。 有些人去散步。 这对我来说很难。 但我最近在车上度过了一段时间，开车几个小时到一个地方，然后几个小时回家，这给了我思考的自由。 我无事可做，但想想那个时候。 它使我能够深入思考。 为什么我会有这种感觉？ 我最大的梦想是什么？ 我如何让这些梦想为他人实现？ 我们需要远离屏幕的时间——尽管这感觉违反直觉。 当我们让大脑休息时，我们就会成长。

毅力：激情和毅力的力量安吉拉·达克沃斯

另一个最近的听。 但这比Deepwork 之类的书更能引起我的共鸣。 我是四个孩子的妈妈。 事情经常很难。 我花时间面试技术职位。 幸运的是，没有那么多，但它很累。 我认为要成功，你必须重新构建你的思维过程：每次采访都像是一场常规赛。 你赢了一些，你输了一些。 你起床再试一次。 你随着每一次经历而成长。 这不是没有意义的。 这是关于成长和推动自己走出舒适区，无论你已经成功还是需要回去再试一次。

在过去的三年里，我经历了很多。 有时我很兴奋能继续前进。 其他时候，我想辞职。 当你一再感到失败时，你如何不放弃？ 你养成习惯。 你这样做，直到你觉得你已经变得更好了。 然后你继续前进。 如果你在一夜之间学会了这件事并不重要。 重要的是你已经学会了这件事。 你如何准备好走出那个舒适区并继续学习？ 这本书设置了步骤。

我读过很多书，但自从我进入科技行业以来，这些是最能引起我共鸣的书。 他们帮助我重新构建了我对失败、成长和联系的理解。 他们是否明确涉及技术？ 不，但我认为这会让他们变得更好。 它们是关于人类经验的。 生活在科技泡沫之外有助于我们更好地看待科技领域正在发生的事情。



原文:

I spent the majority of my career teaching college English. I read so, so much fiction, poetry, plays, and screenplays. And when I moved into tech, I kind of stopped reading for a while. I was dealing with trauma. I was leaving a profession that didn't support me. And I was a mom of 4, and I was tired. I used to think I was a visual learner. But I couldn't sit and read anymore. I hated listening to audiobooks. But when I got my first job in tech, I bought myself a pair of airpods to celebrate. And I discovered that I loved listening to podcasts and audiobooks, but only with headphones. And I've intensely consumed them since then. But my taste has shifted. I've moved into a new industry--actually kind of two: tech and community--so my reading list has reflected that. I'm grateful that our public library has enabled me to seek a path of exploration, an education that suits me and my needs and that enabled this post. Today, I'm going to break down my top five reading resources for new and early career developers.

Radical Candor: Be a Kickass Boss Without Losing Your Humanity by Kim Malone Scott

As a new developer, you're going to receive feedback and you're going to need to know how to give feedback as well. It will frequently be hard. Understanding the importance of feedback, and being honest with empathy and with the intent of action will be incredibly important as you progress. Getting good feedback enables you to grow. Giving good feedback empowers others to grow. This book can help you do both.

Ultralearning: Master Hard Skills, Outsmart the Competition, and Accelerate Your Career by Scott H. Young

I've listened to other books that were similar, but this one stuck with me. Maybe it's because he talks a lot about tech. Maybe it's because the idea of immersing yourself into one thing resonated with me. But it speaks to the career-changes journey and the hardships you'll encounter along the way.

Bored and Brilliant: How Spacing Out Can Unlock Your Most Productive & Creative Self by Manoush Zomorodi

This is one of my more recent listens. It validated an idea I had forgotten. So much of breaking into tech is 24-7 tech. Learning about tech. Hobbies about tech. Side projects about tech. I went to a bootcamp, and even though I did it over a year, my mind was stuck on tech that entire time. And I had a hard time breaking that feeling that I needed to be connected all the time. However, some of my most important, growth-filled experiences have come when I've been away from the computer. Some people go for a walk. This is hard for me. But I've spent time in the car recently, driving a couple of hours to a place and then a couple of hours home, and it's given me the freedom to think. I have nothing to do, but think in that time. And it's enabled me to think deeply. Why do I feel this way? What are my biggest dreams? How do I make those dreams come to life for other people? We need time away from screens--even though it feels counter-intuitive. We grow when we let our brains rest.

Grit: The Power of Passion and Perseverance by Angela Duckworth

Another recent listen. But this resonated with me more than books like Deepwork. I'm a mom of four. Things are hard frequently. I've spent time interviewing for tech roles. Luckily, not that many, but it's exhausting. I think to succeed, you have to reframe your thought process: each interview is like a regular season game. You win some, you lose some. You get up and you try again. You grow with each exeperience. It's not meaningless. It's about growing and pushing yourself out of your comfort zone, whether you've succeeded or need to go back and try again.

Atomic Habits by James Clear

I've worked through a lot over the last three years. And sometimes I was excited to keep pushing forward. Other times, I wanted to quit. How do you not give up when you feel like you've failed, repeatedly? You form habits. And you do it until you feel like you've gotten better. And then you keep going. It doesn't matter if you learn the thing in one night. What matters is that you've learned the thing. How do you prepare yourself to move outside of that comfort zone and keep learning? This book sets up the steps.

I've read a lot of books, but these are the ones that have most resonated with me since I've been navigating the tech industry. They've helped me to reframe my understanding of failure, growth, and connection. Are they explicitly about tech? No, but I think that makes them better. They're about the human experience. And living outside of the tech bubble helps us to bring a better perspective to what's happening in tech.
item.author: bekahhw

item.title: 如何写简历？ | How to write a resume?
item.description:
尚泰 P.

发表于你好开发社区，
我正在准备我的投资组合和简历，以开始申​​请初级开发工作。 我的问题是，我在简历上写了什么？ 我没有任何专业经验，我完成的唯一项目来自 Frontendmentor 和 Udemy 课程。 我会在体验部分列出这些吗？ 此外，如果您的项目没有 100% 正确构建怎么办？ 我问是因为我有一些项目的网页在不同的浏览器中显示不同。

Gayatri Sachdeva - Gayatri Sachdeva - May 11 5月11日

Olubisi Idris Ayinde💡 - Olubisi Idris Ayinde💡 - May 6 5月6日



原文:

Chantae P.

Posted on May 13

Hello DEV community,
I am getting my portfolio and resume ready to begin applying for junior dev jobs. My problem is, what do I put down on my resume? I don't have any professional experience and the only projects I have completed are from Frontendmentor and from Udemy courses. Do I list those under the experience section? And also, what if your projects are not built 100% correctly? I ask because I have some projects where the web pages are displayed differently in different browsers.

Gayatri Sachdeva - May 11

Olubisi Idris Ayinde💡 - May 6
item.author: taepal467

item.title: 终极动机指南 | The Ultimate Motivation Guide
item.description:
生活很艰难。 世界上有太多的干扰，我们面临的挑战和困难太多，如果你还没有，请认为自己很幸运。 这听起来很悲伤，但总是有积极的消极的。 你可以使用你所拥有的，创造一些特别的东西。

习惯的力量

我越练越幸运——加里·普莱尔

你编码的越多，你就会越好。 就像骑自行车一样，一开始你会摔倒很多（我知道我有过），但到 13 岁时，你可能会摔倒。 如果你每天编码 0 分钟，你永远学不会编程，但如果你每天编码 30 分钟，也许几个月到几年，你就会对编程语言有一个扎实的理解。 一致性是打开成功之门的关键。 爱上这个过程。

拥抱失败


如果你从不失败，你就永远学不会。 这是你应该遵守的规则。 你的想法中有四分之一会失败，接受它。 这是我学会生活的东西。 过度思考不会解决任何问题，感到悲伤是可以的。 这种失败可能是成功的垫脚石。 迈克尔乔丹职业生涯投丢了9000次，但他一直在坚持。 不要把它看作是失败，把它看作是你生活中的一个学习点。 失败是最终的老师。

游戏化你的生活


游戏也可能很难。 你可能在电子游戏中死过不止一次。 你可能也花了无数个小时在打磨上。 把生活想象成一场游戏。 你从一个随机的人开始，在一个随机的地方生活，你会发现新的习惯。 也许你仰望斯蒂芬金并想成为畅销书作家。 要做到这一点，你将不得不磨。 你将从一级作家开始，并面临不成功的尝试，但你写的越多，你就会越好，就像我说的，一致性是关键。 假设你身体不好，你想慢跑一英里，如果你开始慢跑，你的耐力会非常低。 如果你越来越多地慢跑，你的耐力就会增加，你就能达到你的目标。 游戏规则很简单，你做的越多，就越容易。 社交也是如此，你在世界上遇到的人越多，你最终就会变得越社交。 您可以使用番茄钟技术或流程来游戏化您在如何编码方面的学习的方法。 心流是一种查看您可以专注多长时间而不会分心的方法。

休息一下


游戏有规则，你在生存模式下玩，所以你必须吃，喝，睡。 尽量不要一次工作超过两个小时。 也知道，时间是有限的，所以首先要处理你的最高优先级。

结论

这些是我保持动力的一些技巧。 对我帮助很大的一件事是结果。 如果你把它打磨出来并最终完成了一个项目，它只会让你有一种很好的感觉，那就是你完成了一些事情。 只要继续专注于你的目标，你就会到达那里。 快乐的黑客。



原文:

Life's hard. There is so many distractions in the world, so many challenges and hardships we faced, and if you didn't yet, consider yourself lucky. It sounds sad and all but there's always a positive to a negative. You can use what you have and create something special.

The power of Habits

The more I practice the luckier get - Gary Player

The more you code, the better you'll be. It's like riding a bike, you'll fall down a lot at first (I know I have), but by the age of 13 you'll probably be popping wheelies. If you code for 0 minutes a day, you will never learn how to code, but if you code for 30 minutes a day, maybe by a few month to a couple years you'll have a solid understanding of the programming language. Consistency is the key to unlock the doors to success. Fall in love with the process.

Embrace Failure


If you never fail, you'll never learn. That's a rule you should follow. 1 out of 4 of your ideas will fail, embrace it. It's something I learned to live by. Overthinking will solve nothing and it's okay to feel sad. That failure could be a stepping stone to success. Michael Jordan missed 9000 shots in his career, but he kept going. Don't look at it as failure, look at it as a learning point in your life. Failure is the ultimate teacher.

Gamify your Life


Games can be hard too. You probably have died more than one time in a video game. You also probably spent countless hours on grinding too. Think of life as a game. You start off as a random person in a random place in life, and you discover new habits. Maybe you look up to Stephen King and want to be a best selling author. To do that your going to have to grind. You'll start off as a level one writer and face unsuccessful attempts but the more you write, the better you'll get, like I said, consistency is key. Let's say your out of shape and you want to jog a mile, if you start off jogging your stamina would be really low. If you jog more and more your stamina will increase, and you'll be able to reach your target. The rules to the game are easy, the more you do something, the easier it becomes. Same applies to socializing, the more people you'll meet out in the world, the more social you'll end up becoming. Ways you can gamify your learning in how to code is using the Pomodoro technique or flow. Flow is a way to see how long can you focus without getting distracted.

Take breaks


There are rules the game, your playing in survival mode, so you'll have to eat, drink, and sleep. Try not to work more than two hours at a time. Also know, time is limited, so work on your highest priority first.

Conclusion

These are some of my tricks that kept me motivated. One thing that helps me a lot, is results. If you grind it out and finally completed a project, it just gives you a great feeling that you accomplish something. Just keeps focusing on your goals, and you'll get there. Happy hacking.
item.author: daveguz97

item.title: 有用的 Linux 命令 | Useful Linux Commands
item.description:
学习 Linux 命令行，就像成为一名出色的钢琴家一样，不是我们在一个下午就能学到的东西。 这需要多年的实践。

自从我从 .NET 切换到 Ruby on Rails 后，我发现自己越来越多地使用终端。 作为一名 Rails 开发人员，我意识到的一件事是您需要对终端有一些基本的能力，因为您将一直使用它。 所以我花了一些时间来熟悉一些基本的 Linux 命令，这篇文章试图总结要点。

这不是一个完整的列表，但随着我了解更多信息，我将尝试在原始博客文章中继续添加此列表。 如果您想详细了解 Linux 操作系统，我强烈推荐 William E. Shotts的 The Linux Command Line, 2nd Edition 。

所以这里有一些命令，作为 Linux/Mac 机器上的开发人员，您可能会发现它们很有用。

文件系统

如果文件不存在， touch创建一个文件； 如果存在则更新时间戳。

使用空格键前进，使用b后退。

通过-n表示行数， head -5 file

在调整窗口大小或命令导致窗口混乱时特别有用。

要将命令的输出发送到文件，请使用> ，它会覆盖文件的现有内容。 要追加，请使用>> 。

要将命令的输出发送到另一个命令的标准输入，请使用管道| 特点。

 head /proc/cpuinfo | tr az

流程

进程是一个正在运行的程序。 每个进程都有一个进程 ID (PID)。 有时计算机会变得迟缓或应用程序将停止响应。

以下是命令行中可用的一些工具，可让我们检查程序在做什么。

要检查特定进程，请在ps命令末尾添加 PID，例如ps u 1234

后台进程

通常，在您运行命令后，您不会在进程完成之前得到提示。 您可以使用&将进程从 shell 中分离出来，并将其作为后台进程运行。 如果要在从远程计算机注销时保持程序运行，请使用nohup命令。

文件模式和权限

确定用户是否可以读取、写入或运行文件。 使用ls -l命令查看权限。

 -rw-rw-r-- 1 ak ak 14 Oct 5 07:00 file_one

最左边的字符： -表示文件， d表示目录。

从左到右，权限代表用户、组和其他。

要修改权限，请使用chmod命令，例如chmod 644 file 。

模式意义用于
用户：读/写； 组，其他：阅读文件
用户：读/写； 组，其他：无文件
用户：读/写/执行； 组，其他：读取/执行目录，程序
用户：读/写/执行； 组，其他：无目录，程序
用户：读/写/执行； 组，其他：执行目录

安全

环境变量

程序使用这些数据来改变它们的运行时行为，例如当应用程序在生产环境中运行时选择不同的数据库，而不是在测试环境中。

设置环境变量

NAME = akshay export NAME echo $NAME

这在本地设置。 要全局设置它，请将其添加到~/.bashrc或~/.zshconfig文件中。

希望有帮助。



原文:

Learning the Linux command line, like becoming an accomplished pianist, is not something that we pick up in an afternoon. It takes years of practice.

The Linux Command Line, William E. Shotts

Since I switched to Ruby on Rails from .NET, I find myself increasingly using the terminal every day. One thing I've realized as a Rails developer is that you need to have some basic competence with the terminal, as you will use it all the time. So I spent some time getting familiar with some basic Linux commands, and this post tries to summarize the essentials.

This is not a comprehensive list, but I will try to keep adding to this list on the original blog post as I learn more. If you want a detailed overview of Linux operating system, I highly recommend The Linux Command Line, 2nd Edition by William E. Shotts.

So here are some of the commands that you might find useful as a developer on a Linux/Mac machine.

File system

cat displays the contents of a file, or concatenates the contents of multiple files.

touch creates a file if it doesn't exist; updates the timestamp if it exists.

grep searches the term client in the provided file.

  • -i for case-insensitive search
  • -n for printing line numbers next to the results. 
grep client -in /etc/ssh/sshd_config

less displays a large file one page at a time. Use the space bar to go forward and b to go back.

file tells the format of a file.

head/tail displays the top or bottom of the file. Pass -n for number of lines, head -5 file

reset re-initializes terminal. Especially useful after resizing the window or if a command results in scrambled window.

I/O Redirection

To send the output of a command to a file, use >, which overwrites the existing content of the file. To append, use >>.

To send the output of a command to the standard input of another command, use the pipe | character.

head /proc/cpuinfo | tr a-z

sort sorts lines of text.

uniq Report or omit repeated lines

grep Print lines matching a pattern

wc Print newline, word, and byte counts for each file

head Output the first part of a file

tail Output the last part of a file

tee Read from standard input and write to standard output and files

Processes

A process is a running program. Each process has a process ID (PID). Sometimes a computer will become sluggish or an application will stop responding.

Here are some of the tools available at the command line that let us examine what programs are doing.

ps lists all the running processes.

  1. ps x Show all of your running processes.
  2. ps ax Show all processes on the system, not just the ones you own.
  3. ps u Include more detailed information on processes.
  4. ps w Show full command names, not just what fits on one line.

To check on a specific process, add the PID at the end of the ps command, e.g. ps u 1234

top displays tasks

jobs lists active jobs

bg places a job in the background

fg places a job in the foreground

kill sends a signal to a process

killall kills processes by name

shutdown shuts down or reboots the system

Background Process

Normally, after you run a command, you don't get the prompt back until the process finishes. You can detach a process from the shell with the & which runs it as a background process. The shell returns immediately with the PID of the process.

If you want to keep a program running when you log out from a remote machine, use the nohup command.

File Modes & Permissions

Determine if a user can read, write, or run the file. View the permissions using ls -l command.

-rw-rw-r-- 1 ak ak  14 Oct  5 07:00 file_one

Leftmost character: - indicates a file, d indicates a directory.

rwx stands for read, write, and execute. From left to right, the permissions stand for a user, group, and other.

To modify the permissions, use the chmod command, e.g. chmod 644 file.

Mode Meaning Used For
644 user: r/w; group, other: read files
600 user: read/write; group, other: none files
755 user: read/write/execute; group, other: read/execute dirs, programs
700 user: read/write/execute; group, other: none dirs, programs
711 user: read/write/execute; group, other: execute dirs

Security

id displays user identity

chmod changes a file’s mode

su runs a shell as another user

sudo executes a command as another user

chown changes a file’s owner

chgrp changes a file’s group ownership

passwd changes a user’s password

Environment Variables

The Linux shell maintains information about the current environment. Programs use this data to change their runtime behavior, e.g. selecting different database when the application is running in the production environment, as opposed to the test environment.

printenv prints part or all of the environment

set sets shell options (show the environment when used without argument)

export exports environment to subsequently executed programs

alias creates an alias for a command (or show all aliases when used without argument)

Set environment variable

NAME=akshay
export NAME
echo $NAME

This sets it locally. For setting it globally, add it in the ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshconfig file.

Hope that helps.
item.author: software_writer

item.title: JS 中的管道，或者 Elm 教我的部分应用程序 | Piping in JS, or what Elm taught me about partial application
item.description:
最近有一些关于管道操作员来到 JS 的讨论。 我对这个提议很兴奋，但只是现在我在 Elm 中学习了一些功能模式。

什么是管道操作员？

管道运算符将一个函数的输出“管道”到另一个函数中。

所以不要写

const result = c ( b ( a ( x )));

或者，我更喜欢这样写：

 const one = a ( x ); const two = b ( one ); const result = c ( two );

我们可以写：

 const result = a ( x ) |> b |> c ;

出于这个原因，我将使用.map()作为探索 JS 中的管道以及我从 Elm 中学到的东西的代表。

让我们从一个基本的.map()示例开始：

 const square = ( n ) => n ** 2 ; console . log ([ 1 , 2 , 3 ]. map ( square )); // [1, 4, 9]

这样做是将square(n)函数应用于数组中的每个项目，并返回一个包含这些平方值的新数组。

这类似于 Elm 中的处理方式：

 List . map square [ 1 , 2 , 3 ]

还有另一种方法可以使用匿名箭头函数在 JS 中编写上面的代码：

 console . log ([ 1 , 2 , 3 ]. map ( n => square ( n )));

起初，这两个可能看起来相似，但它们略有不同。

在第一种方式中，我们说将square(n)函数应用于数组中的每个项目。

第二种方式，我们说应用这个匿名<function> ，它将square(n)函数的结果返回到数组中的每个项目。

第一种语法在函数式语言中很常见； 第二个不是。 我们将在下一节探讨原因。

部分应用

在开始部分应用之前，让我们创建另一个函数，这次是乘法：

 const multiply = ( a , b ) => a * b ;

与 out square(n)函数不同，此函数有两个参数。

让我们尝试将我们的数组乘以 10。使用第一种语法，它看起来像这样：

 console . log ([ 1 , 2 , 3 ]. map ( multiply ( 10 ))); // TypeError: NaN is not a function

这很令人沮丧！ 因为multiply()有两个参数，所以我们不能使用第一个语法。

我们可以。 但是，请使用第二种样式语法：

 console . log ([ 1 , 2 , 3 ]. map ( n => multiply ( 10 , n ))); // [ 10, 20, 30 ]

而且，我们甚至可以使用两种语法将这两个算术函数组合在一起：

 console . log ([ 1 , 2 , 3 ]. map ( square ). map ( n => multiply ( 10 , n ))); // [ 10, 40, 90 ]

但是如果我们想要/需要使用第一种语法（比如在 Elm 中）。 然后我们必须使用Partial Application 。

让我们重构我们的multiply()函数以使用部分应用程序：

 const multiplyPartial = ( a ) => ( b ) => a * b ;

如果你像我一样是一个简单的 JavaScript 开发人员，那可能会伤到你的大脑，让你有点不寒而栗。

第一个函数返回另一个函数，该函数返回两个输入的乘积。

使用部分应用程序，您可以编写这样的函数

const multiplyPartial10 = multiplyPartial ( 10 );

回到我们得到的那个错误，使用部分应用我们可以做：

 console . log ([ 1 , 2 , 3 ]. map ( multiplyPartial ( 10 ))); // [10, 20, 30] // or even console . log ([ 1 , 2 , 3 ]. map ( multiplyPartial10 )); // [10, 20, 30]

同样，函数multiplyPartial(10)返回一个函数，该函数应用于数组的每个元素。

混合类型

在 JavaScript 中，参数是两种不同类型的函数是完全可以的：

 const mixedTypesOne = ( a , b ) => a . toUpperCase () + " " + ( b * 10 ); const mixedTypesTwo = ( a , b ) => b . toUpperCase () + " " + ( a * 10 );

他们都给你：

 console . log ([ 1 , 2 , 3 ]. map ( n => mixedTypesOne ( " This number multiplied by 10 is " , n ))); console . log ([ 1 , 2 , 3 ]. map ( n => mixedTypesTwo ( n , " This number multiplied by 10 is " ))); // [ // 'THIS NUMBER MULTIPLIED BY 10 IS 10', // 'THIS NUMBER MULTIPLIED BY 10 IS 20', // 'THIS NUMBER MULTIPLIED BY 10 IS 30' // ]

无论在mixedTypes类型函数中哪个类型最先出现，使用map()中的箭头语法，我们都可以传入正确的参数。

现在让我们使用部分应用程序重构它们：

 const mixedTypesPartialOne = ( a ) => ( b ) => a . toUpperCase () + " " + ( b * 10 ); const mixedTypesPartialTwo = ( a ) => ( b ) => b . toUpperCase () + " " + ( a * 10 );

运行第一个给出：

 console . log ([ 1 , 2 , 3 ]. map ( mixedTypesPartialOne ( " This number multiplied by 10 is " ))); // [ // 'THIS NUMBER MULTIPLIED BY 10 IS 10', // 'THIS NUMBER MULTIPLIED BY 10 IS 20', // 'THIS NUMBER MULTIPLIED BY 10 IS 30' // ]

但第二个：

 console . log ([ 1 , 2 , 3 ]. map ( mixedTypesPartialTwo ( " This number multiplied by 10 is " ))); // TypeError: b.toUpperCase is not a function

在mixedTypesPartialTwo中，作为b传入的参数是数字，而不是字符串。

所以呢？

如上面的示例所示，管道和部分应用程序并不总是与一些常见的 JavaScript 实践（即具有两个参数的函数）配合得很好。

在 Elm 中，函数只接受一个参数， 1 ，其余部分由部分应用完成。

我为管道运算符感到兴奋，但这确实意味着必须对如何编写代码进行一些不同的思考。 我在这个概念上有点挣扎，所以希望这可以帮助其他人。

  1. 所以从概念上讲，每个函数都接受一个参数。 ↩ ↩



原文:

There has been some recent talk about the pipe operator coming to JS. I'm excited about this proposal but only now that I've struggled a bit learning functional patterns in Elm.

What is a pipe operator?

A pipe operator "pipes" the output of one function into another.

So instead of writing

const result = c(b(a(x)));

Or, as I prefer, writing:

const one = a(x);
const two = b(one);
const result = c(two);

We could write:

const result = a(x) |> b |> c;

JavaScript has something similar with chaining methods like .map(), .filter(), and .reduce().

For that reason, I'll be using .map() as a stand in for exploring piping in JS and what I learned from Elm.

Mapping in JS and Elm

Let's start with a basic .map() example:

const square = (n) => n ** 2;
console.log([1, 2, 3].map(square));
// [1, 4, 9]

What this does is apply the square(n) function to every item in the array, and returns a new array with those squared values.

This is similar to the way things are done in Elm:

List.map square [1, 2, 3]

There is another way to write our code above in JS using an anonymous arrow function:

console.log([1, 2, 3].map(n => square(n)));

At first, these two may seem similar, but they're slightly different.

The .map() syntax is like this:

In the first way, we're saying apply the square(n) function to every item in the array.

The second way, we're saying apply this anonymous <function> which returns the result of the square(n) function to every item in the array.

The first syntax is common in functional languages; the second is not. We'll explore why in the next section.

Partial application

Before getting right into partial application, let's create another function, this time for multiplying:

const multiply = (a, b) => a * b;

Unlike out square(n) function, this function takes two parameters.

Let's try to multiply our array by 10. Using the first syntax, it would look like this:

console.log([1, 2, 3].map(multiply(10)));
// TypeError: NaN is not a function

That's frustrating! Because multiply() takes two arguments, we can't use that first syntax.

We can. however, use the second style syntax:

console.log([1, 2, 3].map(n => multiply(10, n)));
// [ 10, 20, 30 ]

And, we can even combine these two arithmetic functions together using both syntaxes:

console.log([1, 2, 3].map(square).map(n => multiply(10, n)));
// [ 10, 40, 90 ]

But if we wanted/needed to use that first syntax (like in Elm). Then we have to use Partial Application.

Let's refactor our multiply() function to employ partial application:

const multiplyPartial = (a) => (b) => a * b;

If you're a simple JavaScript developer like myself, that probably hurt your brain and caused you to shudder a little.

Instead of two parameters, multiplyPartial is like two functions. The first function returns another function which returns the product of the two inputs.

With partial application, you can write a function like this

const multiplyPartial10 = multiplyPartial(10);

The multiplyPartial10 function can now take the b argument, which returns the product of the two:

multiplyPartial10(4)
// 40

Returning to that error we got, using partial application we can do:

console.log([1, 2, 3].map(multiplyPartial(10)));
// [10, 20, 30] // or even
console.log([1, 2, 3].map(multiplyPartial10));
// [10, 20, 30]

Again, the function multiplyPartial(10) returns a function, and that function is applied to each element of the array.

Mixing Types

In JavaScript, a function where the parameters are two different types is perfectly ok:

const mixedTypesOne = (a, b) => a.toUpperCase() + " " + (b * 10);
const mixedTypesTwo = (a, b) => b.toUpperCase() + " " + (a * 10);

Both of them give you:

console.log([1, 2, 3].map(n => mixedTypesOne("This number multiplied by 10 is", n)));
console.log([1, 2, 3].map(n => mixedTypesTwo(n, "This number multiplied by 10 is")));
// [
// 'THIS NUMBER MULTIPLIED BY 10 IS 10',
// 'THIS NUMBER MULTIPLIED BY 10 IS 20',
// 'THIS NUMBER MULTIPLIED BY 10 IS 30'
// ]

Regardless of which type comes first in the mixedTypes function, using the arrow syntax in map() we can pass in the correct argument.

Now let's refactor them using partial application:

const mixedTypesPartialOne = (a) => (b) => a.toUpperCase() + " " + (b * 10);
const mixedTypesPartialTwo = (a) => (b) => b.toUpperCase() + " " + (a * 10);

And running the first gives:

console.log([1, 2, 3].map(mixedTypesPartialOne("This number multiplied by 10 is")));
// [
// 'THIS NUMBER MULTIPLIED BY 10 IS 10',
// 'THIS NUMBER MULTIPLIED BY 10 IS 20',
// 'THIS NUMBER MULTIPLIED BY 10 IS 30'
// ]

But the second:

console.log([1, 2, 3].map(mixedTypesPartialTwo("This number multiplied by 10 is")));
// TypeError: b.toUpperCase is not a function

In mixedTypesPartialTwo, the the argument passed in as b is a number, not a string.

So what?

As the above example demonstrated, piping and partial application don't always play well with some common JavaScript practices — namely, functions with two parameters.

In Elm, functions only take one argument,1 and partial application does the rest.

I'm excited for the pipe operator, but it does mean having to think a little differently about how to write code. I struggled with this concept a bit, so hopefully this can help others.

  1. So conceptually, every function accepts one argument. ↩
item.author: charlesloder

item.title: 第 35 天：博客改进！ | Day 35: Blog refine!
item.description:
伊瓦德·伊夫·哈比马纳

发表于你好！
所以今天很好，我们为我过去 3 个月一直在从事的团队项目进行了最终演示，并且取得了成功。 我花了一些时间休息，我有机会回到博客。 我刚刚在两篇文章中添加了代码引用，我将着手处理明天将发布的第三篇文章，它将是关于setting up jest and react testing library以测试反应应用程序我改变了计划，我决定使用在我的个人项目中用于样式的材料 UI 我意识到顺风非常适合练习更多的 CSS，但会花费很多时间，所以我将使用材料 UI，这似乎是一个更容易更快的选择

今天我还学习了如何在 react 中使用formik和yup ，我认为它们是我将在接下来的日子里使用的很棒的工具

我只需要睡觉，已经是凌晨 3 点了，我们明天再看看怎么样
签字...



原文:

Ivad Yves HABIMANA

Posted on May 14

Hi!
So today was a good one, we had a final demo for the team project I have been working on for the last 3 months and it was a success. I took some time to rest and I got a chance to get back to the blog. I just added code references on both articles and I'm going to work on the third one that will be published by tomorrow and it will be about setting up jest and react testing library for testing react apps I changed the plan, I decided to use material UI for styling in my personal project I realized tailwind is great for practising more CSS but will take a lot of time so I will go with material UI which seems to be an easier faster option

today I also learned about working with formik and yup in react and I think they are great tools that I will use in the upcoming days

I just need to sleep it's 3am already we will see what about tomorrow later
signing off...

item.title: #12：无头 CMS 和 SWA - 使用 Blazor 构建 | #12: Headless CMS & SWA - Built With Blazor
item.description:
欢迎来到#30DaysOfSWA的Week 2, Day 5天！

在过去的几天里，我们一直在探索基于 JavaScript 的框架来构建 Web 应用程序。 今天，我们将注意力转移到令人惊叹的 .NET 社区，并讨论使用Blazor构建静态 Web 应用程序

我们将涵盖的内容

  • 简介：Blazor WebAssembly
  • 快速入门：简单 Blazor SWA
  • 高级：Blazor SWA + Wordpress CMS
  • 练习：混音 Fritz 的帽子系列！

关于作者

今天的帖子有来自 .NET 社区的两位知名技术专家的贡献。

首先，向 Justin Yoo 打个招呼，他是 .NET Cloud Advocacy 团队的核心成员、Microsoft MVP 校友，也是一位受欢迎的演讲者和作家。 找到他@justinchronicle或在 dev.to 上关注他

接下来，向 Jeff Fritz 打个招呼 - 一位流行的 Twitch直播主 @theLiveCoders ，Microsoft 的项目经理和 KlipTok 的创建者， KlipTok是一个 Blazor 应用程序，它使流媒体剪辑的社交发现和共享变得更加容易。 请在第 4 周回来查看 KlipTok 上的帖子！

当我们考虑 Web 应用程序时，我们通常会想到使用 JavaScript、HTML 和 CSS 进行开发。 事实上，许多阅读本系列的读者在讨论现代 Web 应用程序时可能会认为自己是 JavaScript 开发人员。

如果我告诉你情况并非总是如此呢？ 如果您可以使用 C# 编写 Web 应用程序，并在浏览器内的 .NET 运行时中运行，那会怎样！ 事实证明你可以 - 感谢Blazor 。

什么是 Blazor？

开发人员可以使用 C#（而不是 JavaScript）构建交互式 Web 应用程序，并将体验扩展到具有 .NET 功能的混合桌面和移动应用程序。

什么是 Web 组装？

它提供了一个沙盒运行时，可以在JavaScript 虚拟机（如现代 Web 浏览器中的虚拟机）中实现，以支持 Web 应用程序。

然后，.NET 代码通过浏览器的 JavaScript 沙箱中的 WebAssembly 执行。 了解Blazor Web 应用程序的工作原理。

简单的 Blazor SWA

想了解如何将简单的 Blazor 应用程序部署到 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序？ 我们为您服务！ 按照本教程了解如何将现有 Blazor 应用部署到 Azure 静态 Web 应用。

最终结果将如下所示 - 显示从无服务器 API 返回的天气数据的单页 Blazor Web 应用程序 - 从这个入门应用程序开始，

想尝试一个更复杂的例子吗？ 查看此高级教程（来自 Justin Yoo）。

以下是该帖子的简要介绍：

构建静态网站最流行的场景之一是为我自己或我的组织运行博客网站。 现在，您想将您的 WordPress 博客网站迁移到静态网站，但这看起来并不容易。

如果您仍想使用 WordPress 网站编写内容但只想刷新网站外的 UI 怎么办？ 如果您甚至可以通过 Blazor WebAssembly 使用 C# 怎么办？ 您现在可以使用现有的 WordPress 站点作为真实数据源，并使用您喜欢的方法在单独的实例中构建 UI。 这听起来很有吸引力吗？

与贾斯汀一起完成以下所需的步骤：

  • 将服务的 Wordpress 实例用作无头 CMS
  • 使用 Wordpress 构建 Blazor WebAssembly 应用程序
  • 在 Azure 静态 Web 应用上托管已完成的应用

最终结果是这样的。 不要忘记 **查看 dev.to 背后的博客文章并亲自尝试一下！

练习：重新混合弗里茨的帽子！

希望你已经准备好尝试一些有趣的东西来强化你今天学到的东西。

向（杰夫）弗里茨的非常酷的帽子问好

该项目由Jeff Fritz创建 - 许多 Twitch 直播和流行的Beginners Series To Blazor背后的面孔。

访问已部署的站点

对于本练习：

探索原始存储库中的源文件和 GitHub Actions 文件以了解应用程序的结构。 然后通过使用 #30DaysOfSWA 标记推文与我们分享您部署的项目。

资源

这里有一些资源可帮助您开始 Blazor 之旅



原文:

Welcome to Week 2, Day 5 of #30DaysOfSWA!!

Over the last few days we've been exploring JavaScript-based frameworks for building web apps. Today, we switch our attention to our amazing .NET community and talk about building Static Web Apps with Blazor

What We'll Cover

  • Introduction: Blazor WebAssembly
  • Quickstart: Simple Blazor SWA
  • Advanced: Blazor SWA + Wordpress CMS
  • Exercise: Remix Fritz's Hat Collection!

About the Authors

Today's post has contributions from two well-known technologists in the .NET community.

First, say hello to Justin Yoo - a core member of the .NET Cloud Advocacy team, Microsoft MVP alumnus, and a popular speaker and author. Find him @justinchronicle or follow him here on dev.to

Next, say hello to Jeff Fritz - a popular Twitch livestreamer @theLiveCoders, a Program Manager at Microsoft and the creator of KlipTok, a Blazor application that makes social discovery and sharing of streaming clips, easier. Check back in week 4 for a post on KlipTok!

Blazor WebAssembly

When we think about web applications, we typically think of developing with JavaScript, HTML, and CSS. In fact, many of you reading this series may identify yourselves as JavaScript developers when discussing modern web apps.

What if I told you that was not always the case? What if you could write web applications in C#, running in a .NET runtime inside the browser! Turns out you can - thanks to Blazor.

What is Blazor?

Blazor is a framework for building interactive client-side web UI with .NET. Developers can build interactive web applications using C# (instead of JavaScript) - and extend experiences to hybrid desktop and mobile apps with .NET capabilities.

What is Web Assembly?

Running .NET code inside web browsers is made possible by WebAssembly(wasm) - a binary instruction format and open web standard that is designed as a portable compilation target for programming languages. It provides a sandboxed runtime that can be implemented inside JavaScript virtual machines (like those in modern web browsers) to support web apps.

How does Blazor WebAssembly Work?

Blazor WebAssembly is a framework for building single page app (SPA) that works with all modern web browsers, including mobile versions. Blazor apps are written using Razor components in a .NET runtime. The .NET code is then executed via WebAssembly in the browser's JavaScript sandbox. WebAssembly uses JavaScript interoperability to get full access to browser features. Learn how Blazor web apps work.

Simple Blazor SWA

Want to learn how you can deploy a simple Blazor application to Azure Static Web Apps? We have you covered! Follow this tutorial to learn how to deploy an existing Blazor app to Azure Static Web Apps.

The end result will look something like this - a single page Blazor web application that displays weather data returned from a serverless API - beginning with this starter app,

Blazor SWA and Wordpress

Want to try a more complex example? Check out this advanced tutorial (from Justin Yoo).

Here's a brief introduction from that post:

One of the most popular scenarios to build a static website is to run a blog site for myself or my organisation. WordPress is the most popular service for this purpose. Now, you want to migrate your WordPress blog site to a static website, but it doesn't look easy.

What if you still want to use the WordPress site to write content but only want to refresh the UI outside the site? What if you can even use C# for it through Blazor WebAssembly? You are now able to use the existing WordPress site as the data source of truth and build a UI in a separate instance with your preferred method. Does that sound attractive?

Join Justin as he walks through the steps required to:

  • use the serviced Wordpress instance as a headless CMS
  • build the Blazor WebAssembly app using Wordpress
  • host the completed app on Azure Static Web Apps

The end result is something like this. Don't forget to **check out the dev.to blog post behind this and try it out for yourself!

Exercise: Remix Fritz's Hats!

Hopefully you're ready to try something fun that will reinforce what you learned today.

Say Hello to (Jeff) Fritz's Really Cool Hats

Fritz's Really Cool Hats is an open-source static web site built with Blazor WebAssembly, incorporating C# Azure Functions and Azure Storage, and deployed using Azure Static Web Apps. The project was created by Jeff Fritz - the face behind many Twitch livestreams and the popular Beginners Series To Blazor.

Visit The Deployed Site

For this exercise:

Explore the source and the GitHub Actions files in the original repository to understand how the app is structured. Then share your deployed project with us by tagging a tweet with #30DaysOfSWA.

Resources

Here are some resources to get you started with your Blazor journey into

item.title: 新开发者在这里....超学习？ | new dev here ....ultra learning?
item.description:
发布1！
我正在建立一个博客/网站来记录我在视频游戏方面的高水平之旅。 首先从 Valorant、Apex Legends 和 Starcraft2 开始！

我将构建一个 cms 风格的博客，并使用 Vue 或其他一些运行速度更快、响应速度更快的框架。 将致力于集成一个 twitch 嵌入按钮，用于流式传输我的 twitch 流或 facebook 游戏。 人们将能够在首页上查看我是否在线。 最重要的是，我将记录对这个博客进行编码的内容和无效的内容。

就像一个二买一的交易编码之旅和爬梯之旅！！！！

按照我为自己设定的高期望，我的目标是每周至少更新一次，并记下我学到的东西。 如果不是每周3个帖子？ 然而，它是超学习，所以它的 SORTA 加速和更快。 谢谢你斯科特杨的想法。



原文:

post 1!
I am building a blog/website to document my journey to a very high level in video games. first off starting with Valorant, Apex Legends, and Starcraft2!

I will be building a cms style blog and using either Vue or some other framework that works faster and responsive. Will work on integrating a twitch embed button that streams my twitch stream or facebook gaming. people will be able to see if im online or not on the front home page. on top of that i will document what worked in coding this blog and what didnt.

ITS LIKE A TWO FOR ONE DEAL A JOURNEY OF CODING AND JOURNEY OF CLIMBING THE LADDER!!!!

going by the high expectations i set for myself i aim to at least update once a week and note down what i have learned. if not 3 posts a week? however it is ultra learning so ITS SORTA ACCELERATED AND FASTER. thank you scott young for the idea.

item.title: Web3：我应该使用 Infura / Alchemy 还是运行节点？ | Web3: Should I use Infura / Alchemy or Run a Node?
item.description:
除非您一直生活在一块石头下，否则您会通过博客文章、新闻文章或其他一些来源遇到 Web3 一词。 相信我，Web3 一直在震撼当今的互联网。

一个用户可以控制自己的数据、身份和命运的互联网。

网络层——又名区块链，后端

  • 交互层——又名节点提供者，区块链世界的 AWS

  • 本文重点介绍交互层，特别是与基于 EVM 的区块链相关的内容。

    原因：因为您刚开始 Web3 开发周期，您需要知道为什么第 1 层很重要、谁提供该服务、我是否需要它等等

    有两种方法可以使用交互层：

    1. 使用第三方服务提供商：例如：Infura、Alchemy、QuickNode 或

    2. 运行你自己的节点客户端：例如，Geth

    正如您已经猜到的那样，这两种选择各有利弊。 但对这个主题没有太多分析。

    本文的目的是让您了解利弊，然后帮助您做出决定是随波逐流还是摆脱困境。

    选项 1：使用节点服务提供者

    • 顾名思义，节点服务提供者在幕后为您运行节点客户端，因此您不必这样做。 然后，它的每个客户端都可以参与“使用”该节点（显然，它是共享的）。 听说过规模经济？！？，是的，这就是现在在这里发挥作用的东西。

    • 这些服务提供者通常会提供一个 API 密钥，您可以使用它与节点进行通信。 是的，你所有的 provider.getBalance(0x) 和其他人都在这里。

    • 使用这些节点通常是通过他们的 SDK 即插即用

    • 有各种节点提供商，如 Alchemy、Infura、QuickNode 提供这些服务。

    为了更好地理解它，下表包含一些最受欢迎的节点提供商及其功能。

    有关更多信息，请查看此页面

    使用上述服务提供商的优点

    1. 便于使用; 只需使用他们自己的 SDK 或其他 Web3 JS 库即插即用

    2. 即时可用性

    3. 可用的备份选项（如果一个节点出现故障，另一个节点会出现）

    4. 提供技术支持团队

    5. 无需维护节点软件； 与节点相关的风险

    6. 细分成本法{稍后会详细介绍}

    缺点

    1. 第三方依赖（这与区块链精神完全相反）

    2. 成本（可能是，可能不是？）

    选项 2：运行您自己的节点。

    通过运行我们自己的节点，我们的意思是您的服务器存储了整个区块链。 您可以选择您的节点是否应该在将每个数据块添加到区块链之前对其进行验证（是的，我们谈论的是挖矿，当涉及到以太坊区块链时的工作证明）。

    虽然，运行自己的节点可以被认为是一种很好的做法，但不推荐，因为它的成本和安装过程所花费的时间。

    • 全节点同步可能需要大约一两个月的时间来下载整个区块链

    • 轻节点同步可能需要数周时间

    互联网上有多个客户端，允许您基于 Go、Rust、Typescript 等各种编程语言运行自己的 Node。

    一些客户端示例

    使用上述服务提供商的优点

    1. 你是自己命运的主人。 没有更多，API 密钥，没有更多的购买计划，API 调用限制等

    缺点

    让我们谈谈成本（财务成本和时间成本）

    使用第三方服务提供商的费用

    显然，在“免费套餐”下可以进行的 API 调用数量是有限制的。 目前，Infura 免费层每天允许 100,000 个请求，而 Alchemy 允许 3 亿个请求。

    当然，上述数字都不是小数字，应该是做任何形式的初级开发； 测试； 教程等。但是，一旦您使用 DAPP，它很容易突破上述限制，您将面临来自 Infura / Alchemy 的成本。

    根据最近的一些记录，平均而言， Uniswap发送大约3000 万个请求， Akropolis发送200 万个请求，而Sushiswap 每天发送大约2500 万个请求。

    现在涉及到很多结构。 例如，如果我们正在设计 DAPP，我们不会公开我们的 API 端点，因此，当我们的用户连接到我们的 DAPP 时，他们的钱包会连接到服务提供商。 因此，不要马上担心上述数字。

    但是，平均而言，我们目睹了如果您维护一个后端来分析您的智能合约数据、性能和各种其他因素，您肯定会看到每月大约 200 美元的成本。

    运行自己的节点的成本

    方面 1：财务（易于衡量的因素）

    运行您自己的节点的硬件要求：

    • 至少 2TB 的 SDD（是的，我们具有前瞻性并计划运行全节点）

    • 英特尔 NUC，第 7 代或更高版本（x86 处理器）

    平均而言，根据上述要求，我们了解每月大约花费约600 美元

    方面2：人力成本

    通常需要您的一名工程师每天至少花费 30 分钟来监控节点的性能； 我们也期待在这里和那里出现故障，因此需要更多的时间参与。 这将使我们每月花费约 24 小时，即每月约 1,200 美元。

    结论

    运行我们自己的节点可能会花费您每年 22,000 美元的估计成本，而使用第 3 方服务提供商的估计成本为 2,400 美元/年。

    使用服务提供商可以简化流程并减少整个耗时的说明。 尽管如此，可靠性和其他一些因素确实使运行您自己的节点的情况值得考虑。

    给你！ 现在你有优点和缺点。 现在，世界上大多数 dapp 开发人员选择第三方服务提供商的原因可能已经很明显了。

    但是，您需要了解这两种方法都各自发挥着重要作用，只有您才能确定哪种方法最适合您。

    我们很想知道您的想法； 我们愿意学习，因此如果您认为我们错过了什么； 很高兴知道。

    作者（接受反馈）： Amateur-Dev和Pari Tomar



    • 原文:

    Quick Recap on Web3

    Unless you have been living under a rock, you would have come across the term Web3, through a blog post, news articles, or some other source. Trust me, Web3 has been shaking the internet as it exists today.

    Web3 is the culmination of a vision of serverless internet, the decentralized web. An internet where users are in control of their own data, identity, and destiny.

    The Web3 stack looks something like that

    • Network Layer — aka Blockchain, the backend

    • Interaction Layer — aka Node providers, the AWSes of the blockchain world

    • *Decentralized Applications *— Customer interfaces to access Web 3.0, the “apps”

    This article focuses on the Interaction Layer, specifically in relation to EVM based Blockchains.

    Reason: Cause you are starting out on the Web3 development cycle and you need to know as why is the layer 1important, who provides that service, do I need it, etc etc

    There are 2 approaches to work with the interaction layer:

    1. Using third-party service providers: Eg: Infura, Alchemy, QuickNode, OR

    2. Running your own node client: Eg, Geth

    As you would have already guessed, there are pros and cons to both the options. 90% of the dapp development happens using the option 2; but without much analysis into this subject.

    The objective of this article is to let you know of the pros and cons and then to help you make a decision on should follow the herd or break free.

    OPTION 1: Using A Node Service Provider

    • As the name suggests Node service provider runs node clients behind the scenes for you so that you don’t have to. Each of its clients can then participate in “using” the node (obviously, it is shared). Heard economies of scale?!?, yes that is what comes into play over here now.

    • These service providers typically provide an API key that you can use to communicate with the node. Yes, all your provider.getBalance(0x) and others comes here.

    • Using these nodes is usually a plug and play through their SDK

    • There are various node providers out there like Alchemy, Infura, QuickNode offering these services.

    To understand it in a better manner, the following table consists of some of the most popular node providers and their features.

    For more information, check out this page

    Pros of Using the above mentioned service providers

    1. Easy to Use; just plug and play using their own SDK or other Web3 JS libraries

    2. Instant Availability

    3. Back-up options available (should one node fail, the other comes up)

    4. Technical Support Team available

    5. No hassle of maintaining the Node Software; risks associated with Nodes

    6. Fractionalized Costing {more on this later}

    Cons

    1. Third-party reliance (isn’t that exactly opposite to what blockchain ethos is all about)

    2. Cost (may be, may be not?)

    OPTION 2 :Running Your Own Node.

    By running our own Node we mean that your server stores the entire blockchain. You may choose whether your Node should validate every block of data before adding it to the blockchain (yes yes, we are talking about mining, proof of work when it comes to Ethereum Blockchain).

    Although, running own node can be considered good practice but is not recommended due to its cost and time taken for the installation process.

    • A Full Node sync can take around a month or two for downloading the whole blockchain

    • The Light Node sync can take up to several weeks

    There exist multiple clients on the internet that allow you to run their own Node based on various programming languages like Go, Rust, Typescript, etc.

    Some client examples

    Pros of Using the above mentioned service providers

    1. You are the master of your own destiny. No more, API keys, No more purchase a plan, API Call limits, etc

    Cons

    Lets talk about the Costs (financial and then the time costs)

    Costs of using 3rd party service providers

    Providers like Infura and Alchemy provide a free tier to use their services. Obviously, there are limits to the number of API calls that you can make under the “Free Tier”. Currently, Infura permits 100,000 requests per day in the free tier, while Alchemy permits 300 Million requests.

    Of course, the above-mentioned numbers are not small numbers, should be doing any form of initial stage development; testing; tutorials etc. But as soon as you go live with the a DAPP, which will easily bust the above limits, you are sitting with costs from Infura / Alchemy.

    According to some recent records, on average, Uniswap sends around 30 million requests, Akropolis sends 2 million requests whereas, Sushiswap sends around 25 million requests per day.

    Now there is a lot of structuring involved. For eg, if we are designing a DAPP, we do not make our API endpoints public and hence, when our users connect to our DAPP, their wallet connects to a service provider. Hence, do not fret the above numbers straight away.

    However, on average, we have witnessed that if you are maintaining a backend to analyse your smart contract data, performance and various other factors, you are definitely looking at ~$200 costs per month.

    Costs of running your own node

    Aspect 1: Financial (the easily measurable factor)

    Hardware requirements to run your own node:

    • At least 2TB of SDD (yes, we are forward looking and are planning to run the full node)

    • 4GB Ram (oh common, don’t be cheap with a 2GB ram now)

    • Connected to the internet 24*7

    • Intel NUC, 7th gen or higher (x86 processor)

    On an average, based on the above requirements, we understand that it costs roughly ~600$ a month

    Aspect 2: Manpower Costs

    It would typically require one of your engineers to least spend 30 mins each day just to monitor the performance of the node; we are also expecting a here and there break-down and hence, more time involvement. This will cost us ~24 hours a month, ie, ~ $1,200 per month.

    Conclusion

    Running our own node can cost you an estimated cost of $22K / year, compared to an estimated cost of $2,400 / year on account of using 3rd party service providers.

    Using a service provider can ease out the process and cut the whole time-consuming instructions. Nonetheless, reliability and some other factors do make the case for running your own node worth considering.

    There you go! Now you have the pros and cons. It is, probably, now obvious why majority of the dapp developers in the world choose 3rd party service providers.

    However, you need to understand that both of the approaches play a major role individually, and it only you who can determine which approach suits you best.

    We would love to know your thoughts; we are open to learn and hence if you think we have missed out anything; more than happy to know.

    Authors (open to feedback): Amateur-Dev and Pari Tomar
item.author: a_mature_dev

item.title: Azure 应用服务 | Azure App Services
item.description:
应用程序可以在基于 Windows 和 Linux 的环境中轻松运行和扩展。

它们提供了许多令人惊叹的功能，例如（自动）缩放、简单的身份验证、离线同步（用于移动应用程序）、混合连接等等。

它直接内置于平台中，不需要任何特定的语言、SDK、安全专业知识，甚至不需要任何代码即可使用。 您可以与多个登录提供程序集成。 例如，Azure AD、Facebook、谷歌、Twitter。

认证流程
所有提供商的身份验证流程都相同，但根据您是否要使用提供商的 SDK 登录而有所不同。

部署槽
部署槽是具有自己主机名的实时应用程序。 应用程序内容和配置元素可以在两个部署槽之间交换，包括生产槽。

您可以手动将资源扩展至实例计数，或通过基于指标扩展资源的自定义自动扩展策略。



原文:

Azure App Service is an HTTP-based service for hosting web applications, REST APIs and mobile apps develop in your favorite language, be it .NET, .NET Core, Java, Ruby, Node.js, PHP, or Python. Applications run and scale with ease on both Windows and Linux-based environments.

Azure App Service is a fully managed platform as a service (PaaS) offering for developers. They provide a host of amazing features like (auto)scaling, easy authentication, offline sync (for Mobile Apps), hybrid connections and much more.

Azure App Service allows you to integrate a variety of auth capabilities into your web app or API without implementing them yourself. It’s built directly into the platform and doesn’t require any particular language, SDK, security expertise, or even any code to utilize. You can integrate with multiple login providers. For example, Azure AD, Facebook, Google, Twitter.

Authentication flow
The authentication flow is the same for all providers, but differs depending on whether you want to sign in with the provider's SDK.

DEPLOYMENT SLOT
Deployment slots are live apps with their own hostnames. App content and configurations elements can be swapped between two deployment slots, including the production slot.

SCALE UP (App service plan)
You can scale up your resources manually to an instance count or via custom autoscale policy that scales your resources base on metrics.
item.author: ekemtech

item.title: JavaScript：链接列表（第 1 部分） | JavaScript: Linked Lists (pt. 1)
item.description:
链接列表是一种常见的数据结构，您可能会在求职面试中收到有关它的问题。 让我们从高层次看一下什么是链表。

它是什么？

链表是存储一些数据的元素列表（我们将其称为节点），以及指向列表中其他节点的指针（或多个指针）。 这个指向是创建列表的原因。 链表有两种主要类型：单链表和双链表。

在单向链表中，每个节点只知道链表中的下一个节点。 当我们到达最后一个节点时，它指向 null。 对于双向链表，每个节点都知道列表中的前一个节点和下一个节点。 这允许向前和向后遍历列表，而单链接只能向前遍历。

单链表

双向链表

为什么要这样做？

当我们已经在 J​​avaScript 中内置了数组时，为什么还需要一个链表？ 很好的问题，我也想知道。 两者之间的主要区别在于它们在内存中的存储方式。 让我们来看看每个的优点/缺点。

数组

数组元素存储在连续的内存位置。 这意味着它们的元素都存储在一行中。

链表

链表不需要存储在连续的位置。 由于每个节点都存储下一个节点的位置，因此它们可以存储在任何地方。

数组

优点：
  • 随机访问：由于数组连续存储元素，因此访问给定索引处的元素要快得多。 无需遍历元素，我们已经知道它的位置。
  • 每个元素的内存更少：数组中的元素不需要存储下一个元素的位置。
缺点：
  • 插入/删除：由于数组使用连续内存，所以速度要慢得多，添加新元素需要移动其他元素的内存位置以腾出空间。
  • 静态内存分配：数组的内存发生在编译时。 它不能在运行时动态更改，因为这可能会导致覆盖数据。

链表

优点：
  • 插入/删除：由于链表使用非连续内存，这些操作要快得多。 无需移动任何东西，只需更新节点指向的位置。
  • 动态内存分配：新节点一旦创建就可以在运行时插入。
缺点：
  • 顺序访问：查找链表的给定索引需要遍历列表直到该点，这比数组的随机访问要慢得多。
  • 增加存储：链表节点需要存储下一个节点的位置。 这增加了每个元素使用的存储空间。

链表是我们称为节点的元素的数据结构。 每个节点存储下一个节点的位置，这会创建一个列表。 虽然类似于数组，但它具有更快插入/删除的优点，并且可以在运行时动态修改。 相比之下，缺点是访问速度较慢和存储使用量增加，因为每个节点都存储下一个节点的位置。



原文:

Linked lists are a common data structure that you may receive questions about during a job interview. Let's take a look at what a linked list is from a high level.

What is it?

A linked list is a list of elements (which we'll refer to as nodes) that store some data, as well a pointer (or pointers) to other nodes in the list. This pointing is what creates the list. There are two major types of linked lists: singly and doubly linked lists.

In a singly linked list, each node only knows about the next node in the list. When we reach the last node it points to null. For doubly linked lists each node knows about the previous and next node in the list. This allows traversing of the list forwards and backward, whereas singly linked can only be traversed forwards.

Singly Linked List

Doubly Linked List

Why do this?

Why do we need a linked list when we already have array's built right into JavaScript? Great question, I wondered the same. The major difference between the two is how they are stored in memory. Let's take a look at the pros/cons of each.

Arrays

Arrays elements are stored in contiguous memory locations. Meaning that their elements are stored all in a row.

Linked Lists

Linked lists do not need to be stored in contiguous locations. Due to each node storing the location of the next node, they can be stored anywhere.

Arrays

Pros:
  • Random access: since arrays store elements contiguously, accessing an element at a given index is much quicker. (ex. array[3]). No need to traverse the elements, we already know its location.
  • Less memory per element: an element in an array does not need to store the location of the next element.
Cons:
  • Insertion/Deletion: is much slower because arrays use contiguous memory, adding a new element requires shifting the memory locations of other elements to make room.
  • Static Memory Allocation: memory for an array takes place at compile time. It can not be altered dynamically at runtime because that may result in overwriting data.

Linked Lists

Pros:
  • Insertion/Deletion: since linked lists use non contiguous memory, these operations are much faster. There's no need to shift anything around, just update where the nodes point.
  • Dynamic Memory Allocation: new nodes can be inserted at runtime as soon as they are created.
Cons:
  • Sequential Access: finding a given index of a linked list requires traversing the list up to that point, this is much slower than the random access of an array.
  • Increased storage: linked list nodes need to store the location of the next node. This increases the storage used per element.

TL;DR

A linked list is a data structure of elements we refer to as nodes. Each node stores the location of the next and this creates a list. While similar to an array it has the advantages of faster insertion/deletion, and can be modified dynamically at run time. The disadvantages in comparison are slower access and increased storage usage, due to each node storing the location of the next.
item.author: seantansey

item.title: Una actualización sobre los components independientes | Una actualización sobre los componentes independientes
item.description:
单组件角度模块

  • 例如，在 LazyPageModule 和LazyPageModule的LazyPageRoutingModule ，可以使用LazyPageComponent le faltan 依赖项。

    对 API loadComponent propuesta para la carga diferida de componentes independientes individuales 的详细信息，请参阅 RFC 的更多信息。

    ¿Qué hay en el segundo RFC? ¿ Qué hay en el segundo RFC？

    En los últimos meses, hemos centrado nuestros esfuerzos en la cuestión de cómo los componentes independientes encajan en el resto de Angular y cómo se pueden usar para lograr casos de uso específicos sin necesidad de recurrir a NgModules. En los últimos meses, hemos centrado nuestros esfuerzos en la cuestión de cómo los components independientes encajan en el resto de Angular y cómo se pueden usar para lograr casos de uso específicos sin necesidad de recurrir a NgModules。 El segundo RFC propone nuevas API que se integran con componentes independientes para admitir casos de uso importantes que anteriormente requerían NgModules: El segundo RFC propone nuevas API que se integran con components independientes para admissionir casos de uso importantes que anteriormente requerían NgModules：

    • Hacer Bootstrapping de una aplicación Hacer Bootstrapping de una aplicación

    • Cargar diferidamente componentes y/o rutas adicionales a través del enrutador Cargar diferidamente components y/o rutas adicionales a través del enrutador

    • Crear instancias dinámicamente de componentes en tiempo de ejecución Crear instancias dinámicamente de components en tiempo de ejecución

    Además, el RFC cubre otras API relacionadas con el objetivo más amplio de reducir la necesidad de escribir NgModules, que incluyen: Uso de NgModules existentes (como los de las bibliotecas) en las API existentes Además, el RFC cubre otras API relacionadas con el objetivo más amplio de reducir la necesidad de escribir NgModules, que incluyen: Uso de NgModules existentes (como los de las bibliotecas) en las API existentes

    Proporcionar lógica de inicialización en varios contextos, previamente soportada por NgModules Proporcionar lógica de inicialización en varios contextos, previamente soportada por NgModules

    • ¿Qué desafíos hemos encontrado? ¿ Qué desafíos hemos encontrado？

    El tamaño del código siempre es una consideración importante al agregar cualquier característica nueva a Angular, incluido el proyecto independiente. El tamaño del código siempre es una Consideración importante al agregar cualquier característica nueva a Angular, incluido el proyecto independiente。 Pasamos mucho tiempo pensando en el impacto que tendrán las nuevas características en los tamaños de los paquetes y cómo minimizar ese impacto en casos comunes. Pasamos mucho tiempo pensando en elimpacto que tentrán las nuevas características en los tamaños de los paquetes y cómo minimizar ese Impacto en casos comunes。 A menudo, estas consideraciones van en ambos sentidos: las aplicaciones existentes que aún utilizan API más antiguas no deberían necesitar enviar código asociado con las nuevas, pero los usuarios de API más nuevas tampoco deberían necesitar enviar código asociado con las más antiguas. A menudo, estas thinkaciones van en ambos sentidos: las aplicaciones existentes que aún utilizan API más antiguas no deberían necesitar enviar código asociado con las nuevas, pero los usuarios de API más nuevas tampoco deberían necesitar enviar código asociado con las más antiguas。

    Por ejemplo, las aplicaciones que emplean la nueva API de arranque independiente propuesta no deberían necesitar enviar el código asociado con el sistema de arranque anterior basado en NgModule. Por ejemplo, las aplicaciones que emplean la nueva API de arranque independiente prouesta no deberían necesitar enviar el código asociado con el sistema de arranque anterior basado en NgModule。 Hacer que esto funcione de manera óptima es un desafío, ya que las API más antiguas no necesariamente se han diseñado teniendo en cuenta estas consideraciones. Hacer que esto funcione de manera óptima es un desafío, ya que las API más antiguas no necesariamente se han diseñado teniendo en cuenta estas thinkaciones。

    ¿Cómo cambiará esto la guía de estilo de Angular? ¿Cómo cambiará esto la guíade estilo de Angular？

    A corto plazo, no planeamos actualizar la guía de estilo de Angular ni la aplicación predeterminada de ng new . A corto plazo, no planeamos actualizar la guíade estilo de Angular ni la aplicación predeterminada de ng new 。 Los componentes independientes estarán disponibles por primera vez como una función opcional. Los components independientes estarán disponibles por primea vez como una función opcional。
    A más largo plazo, recopilaremos comentarios de los desarrolladores de nuestra comunidad a medida que usen las nuevas API y usaremos esos comentarios para revisar tanto la guía de estilo como la experiencia ng new . A más largo plazo, recopilaremos comentarios de los desarrolladores de nuestra comunidad a medida que usen las nuevas API y usaremos esos comentarios para revisar tanto la guía de estilo como la experiencia ng new .

    ¿Qué tan avanzado está el proyecto? ¿ Qué tan avanzado está el proyecto? ¿Cuándo puedo utilizar componentes independientes? ¿ Cuándo puedo utilizar components independientes？

    La implementación de la funcionalidad central de los componentes independientes ya está en marcha y hemos comenzado a crear prototipos de las API propuestas que se describen en el nuevo RFC . La implementación de la funcionalidad central de los components independientes ya está en marcha y hemos comenzado a crear prototipos de las API propuestas que se describen en el nuevo RFC 。 A medida que recibamos comentarios, perfeccionaremos esas implementaciones. A medida que recibamos comentarios, perfeccionaremos esas implementaciones。

    Dado que toda la funcionalidad de los componentes independientes y las API asociadas son opcionales y no se rompen, los componentes independientes no tienen que esperar a una versión principal; Dado que toda la funcionalidad de los components independientes y las API associadas son opcionales y no se rompen, los componentes independientes no tienen que esperar a una version principal; podremos lanzar esta nueva función emocionante en una próxima versión secundaria de Angular tan pronto como esté listo. podremos lanzar esta nueva función emocionante en una próxima 版本 secundaria de Angular tan pronto como esté listo。 ¡Así que permanezca atento a medida que continuamos progresando y asegúrese de leer el RFC ! ¡ Así que permanezca atento a medida que continuamos progresando y asegúrese de leer el RFC ！

    Goreto Educational Consultancy - Goreto 教育咨询 - Apr 20 4月20日



    原文:

    (¡y directivas y pipes!)

    Artículo original de Angular Blog por Alex Rickabaugh en inglés aquí:

    Durante el artículo encontrarás la RFC(Request for Comments) o Solicitud de comentarios, Carga diferida se refiere (lazily) o lazy loading.

    Hace unos meses se publicó el primer RFC relacionado con un proyecto conocido como "componentes independientes". Este proyecto tiene un objetivo ambicioso: reducir el modelo estándar y hacer que Angular sea más fácil de usar y aprender al hacer posible la creación de componentes y aplicaciones sin NgModules.

    Unas semanas, después, se construyo sobre esa base con un segundo RFC que analiza cómo los componentes independientes se integrarán en el resto del marco Angular. En esta publicación, compartiremos una actualización del proyecto, algunas lecciones aprendidas hasta ahora y lo que los desarrolladores pueden esperar en el futuro cercano.

    ¿Por qué componentes independientes?, ¿Qué tiene de malo los NgModules?

    Los componentes independientes ayudarán a:

    • Reducir el modelo estándar en muchas aplicaciones, especialmente para aquellas que ya utilizan patrones arquitectónicos como el patrón de módulo angular de componente único (SCAM). Single Component Angular Module

    • Facilitar el aprendizaje de Angular eliminando el concepto NgModule de la curva de aprendizaje crítica de los desarrolladores nuevos en Angular

    • Permitir ofrecer nuevas API y más optimizadas para casos de uso comunes

    Un requisito común en una aplicación es cargar de forma diferida una ruta específica. Lograr esto hoy es bastante detallado. Para cargar de forma diferida una ruta a LazyPageComponent, por ejemplo:

    Esto es mucho código para escribir y entender. También es demasiado fácil cometer errores sutiles. Por ejemplo, si olvida importar LazyPageModule a LazyPageRoutingModule, es posible que esta ruta no funcione en tiempo de ejecución si a LazyPageComponent le faltan dependencias.

    Con las nuevas API que aprovechan las garantías proporcionadas por los componentes independientes, lo anterior se puede reducir a:

    Para obtener más detalles sobre la API loadComponent propuesta para la carga diferida de componentes independientes individuales, obtenga más información en el nuevo RFC.

    ¿Qué hay en el segundo RFC?

    En los últimos meses, hemos centrado nuestros esfuerzos en la cuestión de cómo los componentes independientes encajan en el resto de Angular y cómo se pueden usar para lograr casos de uso específicos sin necesidad de recurrir a NgModules. El segundo RFC propone nuevas API que se integran con componentes independientes para admitir casos de uso importantes que anteriormente requerían NgModules:

    • Hacer Bootstrapping de una aplicación

    • Cargar diferidamente componentes y/o rutas adicionales a través del enrutador

    • Crear instancias dinámicamente de componentes en tiempo de ejecución

    Además, el RFC cubre otras API relacionadas con el objetivo más amplio de reducir la necesidad de escribir NgModules, que incluyen: Uso de NgModules existentes (como los de las bibliotecas) en las API existentes

    Proporcionar lógica de inicialización en varios contextos, previamente soportada por NgModules

    ¿Qué desafíos hemos encontrado?

    El tamaño del código siempre es una consideración importante al agregar cualquier característica nueva a Angular, incluido el proyecto independiente. Pasamos mucho tiempo pensando en el impacto que tendrán las nuevas características en los tamaños de los paquetes y cómo minimizar ese impacto en casos comunes. A menudo, estas consideraciones van en ambos sentidos: las aplicaciones existentes que aún utilizan API más antiguas no deberían necesitar enviar código asociado con las nuevas, pero los usuarios de API más nuevas tampoco deberían necesitar enviar código asociado con las más antiguas.  

    Por ejemplo, las aplicaciones que emplean la nueva API de arranque independiente propuesta no deberían necesitar enviar el código asociado con el sistema de arranque anterior basado en NgModule. Hacer que esto funcione de manera óptima es un desafío, ya que las API más antiguas no necesariamente se han diseñado teniendo en cuenta estas consideraciones.

    ¿Cómo cambiará esto la guía de estilo de Angular?

    A corto plazo, no planeamos actualizar la guía de estilo de Angular ni la aplicación predeterminada de ng new. Los componentes independientes estarán disponibles por primera vez como una función opcional.
    A más largo plazo, recopilaremos comentarios de los desarrolladores de nuestra comunidad a medida que usen las nuevas API y usaremos esos comentarios para revisar tanto la guía de estilo como la experiencia ng new.

    ¿Qué tan avanzado está el proyecto? ¿Cuándo puedo utilizar componentes independientes?

    La implementación de la funcionalidad central de los componentes independientes ya está en marcha y hemos comenzado a crear prototipos de las API propuestas que se describen en el nuevo RFC. A medida que recibamos comentarios, perfeccionaremos esas implementaciones.

    Dado que toda la funcionalidad de los componentes independientes y las API asociadas son opcionales y no se rompen, los componentes independientes no tienen que esperar a una versión principal; podremos lanzar esta nueva función emocionante en una próxima versión secundaria de Angular tan pronto como esté listo. ¡Así que permanezca atento a medida que continuamos progresando y asegúrese de leer el RFC!

    Goreto Educational Consultancy - Apr 20
item.author: antoniocardenas

item.title: 开发人员应了解招聘神经多样性人才的 4 件事 | 4 Things Developers Should Know About Recruiting Neurodiverse People
item.description:
我们想让 Dev Interrupted 播客成为您一周中至关重要的、令人愉快的部分。 请花 2 分钟时间回答我们新的听众调查。 它让我们了解了一些关于你的信息，你想从 Dev Interrupted 中得到什么，以及你从播客中得到什么！

本文是由 Lewis Dowling 为Dev Interrupted撰写的。

招聘具有神经多样性的开发人员可能具有挑战性，尤其是对于招聘经验较少的小型公司而言。 这并不是因为你需要一个全新的流程，或者神经多样性的人本来就很难面试。 就是你招聘过程中的小缺陷会加剧。 导致神经典型的人绊倒的障碍，成为神经多样化的人的彻底障碍。

因此，我们请了 UW Medicine 的数据工程经理 Matt Nigh 就如何确保您的招聘流程适合所有人提供他的建议。

如果你使用大量正式的语言、行话和不必要的复杂词汇，你会让你的受访者更难理解你想让他们做什么。 这也使面试变得人工和冷漠，这可能会给你的面试者带来不必要的压力和焦虑。 这对每个人都是如此，但对于神经多样性开发人员来说，它可能更有效。

他们非常擅长解释他们在问什么以及他们在寻找什么。 对我来说，这是一个非常友好的过程。” 在给出指示时，尤其是在实际测试中，确保你的内容清晰明了是很重要的。 留下歧义可能会导致问题，尤其是对于神经多样性开发人员而言。 这种模棱两可会分散手头实际任务的注意力。 您的说明越清晰，您就越能测试开发人员的实际技能。

我花了一半的时间试图理解个人使用的语言，而不是解决问题，试图确保我们与我们所说的内容一致，”马特说。 每个人都有自己的偏见。 虽然我们都应该努力克服这些，但这并不总是可能的。 避免这些问题的最好方法是确保你的面试团队是多元化的。 一些应对机制和策略一开始对于神经典型的招聘人员来说似乎很奇怪。

例如，患有多动症的人可能会要求您在说话时重复要点或打字。 虽然最初看起来他们正在回复电子邮件或没有关注您，但他们更有可能正在做笔记以确保他们正确遵循您的指示。 您的招聘人员越多样化，您做出的错误假设就越少。

比如说，在谷歌，他们有非常包容的招聘人员。 例如，我是被一个聋人招募的。 所以这个人非常清楚地了解我以及正在发生的任何事情。”-在 25:13 的 Dev Interrupted Podcast 上

根据哈佛商业评论的一篇文章，该计划的初步结果似乎表明，这些测试团队的生产力比其他团队高 30%，该文章称神经多样性是一种竞争优势。

看起来，虽然一个神经多样性的人可能在某些领域挣扎——比如面试带来的社交焦虑——但他们可能会在其他方面超越，比如模式识别。

观看完整的采访

如果您想从 Matt 那里听到更多关于软件开发中神经多样性的信息，您可以在我们的频道上观看完整的播客。

渴望顶级软件工程内容？ 需要一些关于如何管理团队的好技巧？ 本文的灵感来自Dev Interrupted - 工程领导者的首选播客。

立即收听并订阅您选择的流媒体服务。



原文:

We want to make the Dev Interrupted podcast a vital, enjoyable part of your week. Please take 2 minutes and answer our new Listener Survey. It lets us know a bit about you, what you want from Dev Interrupted and what you want from podcasts in general!

This article was written for Dev Interrupted by Lewis Dowling.

Hiring neurodiverse developers can be challenging, particularly for smaller companies that are less experienced at hiring. This isn’t because you need an entirely new process or that neurodiverse people are inherently trickier to interview. It’s that small flaws in your hiring process get exacerbated. Obstacles that cause neurotypical people to stumble, become outright blockers to a neurodiverse person.

So we asked Matt Nigh, data engineering manager at UW Medicine, to give his tips on how to make sure your hiring process suits everybody.

“I think there are companies that other organizations could mimic,” Matt explained. “I would look at Google as one of probably the best that I’ve experienced.”-On the Dev Interrupted Podcast at 25:50

1. Interview processes should be conversational

If you use a lot of formal language, jargon and needlessly complicated words, you’ll make it much harder for your interviewee to understand what you want them to do. It also makes the interview artificial and cold, which can lead to unnecessary stress and anxiety in your interviewee. This is true for everybody, but for a neurodiverse developer, it can be much more potent.

“The most inclusive interview process I ever experienced was at Google,” Matt said. “And the reason I felt they had such an inclusive process is that it was wildly conversational. They were incredibly good at explaining what they were asking and what they were looking for. And to me, it was an incredibly friendly process.” -On the Dev Interrupted Podcast at 24:10

2. Neurodiverse developers prefer straightforward and clear instructions

When giving instructions, particularly in practical tests, it’s important to make sure that you’re being clear and straightforward. Leaving ambiguity can cause problems, especially for neurodiverse developers. That ambiguity can distract away from the actual task at hand. The clearer your instructions, the better you’ll test a developer’s actual skills.

“I would say the reason I failed the system design interview was (and this is an example of what autism will do during an interview) it was the first system design interview I ever had. And I spent half the time trying to understand the language that the individual was using, rather than solving the problem, trying to make sure we’re just on the same page with what we were saying,” Matt said. -On the Dev Interrupted Podcast at 24:40

3. Neurodiverse developers need diverse recruiters, and stick around for longer once hired

Everyone has their own biases. While we should all strive to overcome those, it’s not always possible. The best way to avoid those problems is to make sure your interview team is diverse. Some coping mechanisms and strategies can seem strange to a neurotypical recruiter at first.

For example, someone with ADHD might ask you to repeat points or be typing as you speak. While it could initially look like they’re answering emails or not paying attention to you, it’s more likely that they’re taking notes to make sure they follow your instructions properly. The more diverse your recruiters, the fewer false assumptions you’ll make.

“Most recruiters are used to looking at neurotypical applicants, and they essentially have mental flags that come up with certain things, certain questions or anything like that,” Matt said. “Companies should ask: Do I have inclusive recruiters? So say, for example, at Google, they had incredibly inclusive recruiters. I was recruited by a deaf individual, for example. So this person very clearly understands me and anything that was going on.”-On the Dev Interrupted Podcast at 25:13

4. Neurodiverse developers could be more productive, and worth changing your processes

A program at Hewlett Packard Enterprise hired over 30 neurodiverse people in software testing roles at Australia’s Department of Human Services. The initial results from the program seem to suggest that those testing teams are 30% more productive than others, according to an article in the Harvard Business Review, called neurodiversity as a competitive advantage.

It would seem that, while a neurodiverse person might struggle in some areas—like the social anxiety brought on by an interview—they could exceed in others, such as pattern recognition.

Watch the full interview

If you’d like to hear more from Matt on neurodiversity in software development, you can watch the full podcast on our channel.

Starved for top-level software engineering content? Need some good tips on how to manage your team? This article is inspired by Dev Interrupted - the go-to podcast for engineering leaders.

Dev Interrupted features expert guests from around the world to explore strategy and day-to-day topics ranging from dev team metrics to accelerating delivery. With new guests every week from Google to small startups, the Dev Interrupted Podcast is a fresh look at the world of software engineering and engineering management.

Listen and subscribe on your streaming service of choice today.
item.author: conorbronsdon

item.title: 为什么……在这里？ | Why... here?
item.description:
这周差点没赶上。 上周末，Covid 终于炸毁了房子，没有人不受影响。 老实说，很惊讶花了这么长时间。 不过，我们现在都做得更好了。

由于我的一周都花在了学习、抗击疾病和在办公室进行了为期 3 天的培训，因此我将在Clint Gibler的tl;dr时事通讯中分享一些我发现的有趣内容。 如果你不熟悉，我强烈推荐他的内容，和其他人一样。


在 Clint 的时事通讯中很容易找到能激起好奇心的东西，但这个特别的项目立即提醒我 a) 我这周没有发表任何东西 b) 我想我应该继续下去是有正当理由的。 我一直在想，由于我的云简历项目基本上是从进度记录的角度完成的，我是否会继续在 DEV 社区发帖。 同样，我最初来到这里的原因只是因为这是该挑战的一个特定要点。

然后我阅读了Jack Rhysider的推文，详细说明了你应该写博客的 17 个原因（是的，你）。

虽然我并不觉得他列出的所有理由都能引起我个人的共鸣，但总的来说，他提出了很好的观点。 来到这里，把我经常随意的想法发表给全世界看，让我有点不安……有点不舒服。

这正是我知道我应该继续的原因。 我从没想过将这个论坛用作我的个人技术笔记本，我也不想向任何人证明我知道任何事情。 但这是一种不同的氛围，完全不同的技能，而且（我承认）我很喜欢它。

所以，我的博客朋友们，继续写吧！

而且，一如既往，感谢您的阅读=]



原文:

Almost didn't make it this week. Covid finally blew threw the house last weekend, leaving no one unaffected in its wake. Surprised it took this long, to be honest. We're all doing better now though.

Being that my week has been spent studying, fighting illness and a 3-day training stint at the office, i'll share something i found interesting in the tl;dr newsletter from Clint Gibler. If you're not familiar, i highly recommend his content, as do others.

 

It's easy to find something to pique one's curiosity in Clint's newsletter, but this particular item immediately reminded me a) i hadn't published anything this week and b) i guess there are valid reasons why i should continue. I have been wondering, as my cloud resume project is basically done from a progress-recording perspective, whether i would continue to post here in the DEV Community. Again, my reason for being here in the first place was only because it was a specific bullet point of that challenge.

Then i read Jack Rhysider's tweet detailing 17 reasons why you should be blogging (yes, you).

While i didn't feel every reason he listed resonated with me personally, overall he makes very good points. Something about being here, publishing my often haphazard thoughts for the world to see, makes me a little uneasy... a little uncomfortable.

And that's exactly why i know i should continue. I don't ever expect to use this forum as my personal technical notebook, and i'm not trying to prove to anyone that i know anything. But this is a different vibe, a different skill set altogether, and (i admit) i do enjoy it.

So, my blogging friends, keep writing!

And, as always, thanks for reading =]
item.author: zwilson

item.title: 作为程序员的毅力，对梦想和目标的承诺 | The perseverance, commitment to my dreams and goals as a programmer
item.description:
我厌倦了混乱，部门和客户之间的冲突（因为许多经理和主管都专注于钱）。 我有一台笔记本电脑（联想ideapad 330s）。 我组装了一张小桌子，拿了一把塑料椅子，一个便宜的 rgb 键盘/鼠标，那是我的第一个工作空间。

嘿！ 这比床好。 相信我！

后来，到那年 11 月（仍然是 2020 年），我从我的第一个自由职业者客户那里得到了一些钱。 我用这笔钱从朋友那里买了一台新显示器和一张奇怪的桌子。 那张桌子很奇怪，因为其中一张桌子展开了，你有一个 L 表工作区。 哦！ 我买了便宜的无线键盘、鼠标和耳机。 我处于“无线”趋势模式。 目前效率不高。 请忽略底部的白色外壳。 那是我的第一台 linux 服务器。 我还买了一把符合人体工程学的椅子。

我使用该设置一年。 到 2021 年底，我将我的电脑从笔记本电脑升级到了便宜的游戏电脑：

  • 锐龙 3 3300X
  • 我是一个小型UPS

我还升级了我的键盘、鼠标和耳机：

  • 带 Outemu 红色开关的键盘 Kumara k552。
  • 鼠标罗技g102
  • 一个 razer 耳机（抱歉，我现在没有型号）。

我很高兴，因为我可以更好地工作，并以更好的图形玩同样的老游戏。

一周后，我终于为我的 kumara 键盘买了一个不错的办公桌 CubiCubi 和酷炫的黑/绿键帽。 我还买了一些便宜的 rgb 扬声器。

到 2022 年 4 月底，我更换了扬声器：买了一个带低音炮的罗技扬声器、一个 LED 灯带和一盏台灯。 这是我目前的设置：

底线是：不知何故，我目前的设置是我在 2020 年开始作为自由职业者工作时的想象。而且我知道我会不断改善我的工作空间和房间。 例如，我将在下周粉刷房间。

我真的很想分享这个，因为我想告诉你，如果你有专注、信念和意志，你就能实现你的目标！



原文:

In 2020 I quit my job as a programmer in a company I was working for 8 years. I got tired of the mess, the conflicts between the departments and clients (because many managers and supervisors are focused on the money). I had a laptop (a Lenovo ideapad 330s). I assembled a small table, took a plastic chair, a cheap rgb keyboard/mouse and that was my first workspace.

Hey! It was better than the bed. Trust me!
